plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate risk.
Trim high-beta software (CRWD) and sell semiconductors (SMH) to reduce concentration.
Increase allocation to income (QQQI) and broad index (SPY) for stability.
Add a high-scoring software name (ZS) for controlled growth.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with reduced leverage.
It aims for lower volatility and a modest monthly return target.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leveraged ETFs and high-beta tech, while building a cash buffer through index and income holdings. Expect lower volatility but also capped upside.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and sell the riskiest (SPAL).
Reduce semiconductor exposure (SMH) while adding a quality name (ASML).
Increase broad index (SPY) and add to strong tech (CRWD, NVDA).
This blend balances growth with risk reduction.
It aims for a moderate monthly return target of around 2.5%.
Concentration and leverage are managed to stay within limits.
balanced.read: This plan seeks risk-first growth with a target of around 2.5% per month. It trims leverage and concentration while adding quality tech and semiconductor names. Expect moderate growth potential with controlled drawdowns. Leverage is kept in check to manage risk.

plan: Increase leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) for amplified gains.
Add to semiconductors (SMH) and top tech (CRWD).
Initiate positions in high-conviction scanner setups (ZS, NVDA).
This plan aims for aggressive growth with a target of around 4% per month.
It leans into concentration and leverage, increasing both upside and potential drawdown.
Leveraged ETFs decay daily; strict stop discipline is critical.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets around 4% per month by increasing leverage and concentrating in high-scoring setups, including leveraged ETFs. Expect the highest potential upside but also the largest potential drawdowns and risk of significant losses. Leveraged ETF decay is a constant factor.

plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs and volatile tech/semiconductor holdings.
Significantly increase allocation to income-generating assets (QQQI).
Add broad index (SPY) for stability and a dividend-paying stock (XOM).
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions over growth.
It aims for a low target return of around 1% per month with minimal drawdown.
Focus is on capital preservation and reliable income generation.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% per month by prioritizing steady distributions and capital preservation. It eliminates high-beta and leveraged assets, focusing on income ETFs and stable broad index exposure. Expect low volatility and reliable income, but capped growth potential.
